title: ervatamia oligantha
intro: Ervatamia oligantha, also known as the Lesser Thaum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to tropical regions in Southeast Asia, and its roots, leaves, and stems are used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. Ervatamia oligantha has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
